marketState: neutral-to-bullish — weather-driven upside risk vs. storage-surplus headwind
summary: NG front-month is trading near $3.27, essentially flat day-over-day, and is 'SLIGHTLY OVER' our fair-value model at every horizon ($2.93 now rising to $3.09 by +21D) but still comfortably inside the fair-value bands. The standout signal is weather: 8-16 day cooling demand is running +32% above the 5-year norm and above the historical max of its range (up sharply, +23.8 in 24h), with 1-7 day demand also elevated (+11.5%), a genuine bullish catalyst the market may not have fully priced. Seasonality tilts modestly bullish (56-63% probability of higher prices over 1-4 weeks, YTD outperforming the median) but reliability is explicitly flagged Low (51pp spread). Working against the bulls: storage sits +175 Bcf above the 5-year average with a bearish +13 Bcf build surprise, and daily/weekly technicals still show a bearish EMA structure (50<200) even though intraday (5m-4h) trend and RSI are bullish. Managed money is net short but only at the 31st percentile of its 5-year range, leaving room for short-covering if the weather-driven demand spike plays out.

longTerm.rationale: 8-16D cooling demand is +32% vs 5y and above its historical max range, seasonality favors higher prices into weeks 3-4 (63% probability, +2.3% typical 4wk move), and the fair-value curve itself rises from $2.93 now to $3.09 by +21D — price is only slightly rich today and gets 'less rich' as fair value catches up, leaving room to run toward the Dec26 curve premium ($3.94).
longTerm.invalidation: A shift to below-normal 8-16D demand forecasts, or storage surprises turning consistently bearish (large builds vs forecast), would remove the demand catalyst and confirm the bearish weekly/daily EMA structure instead.

shortTerm.rationale: Intraday technicals (5m/15m/1h/4h) are all trend-bullish with RSI 54-60 (not overbought, mostly positive MACD aside from a slightly negative 1h reading), and 1-7D weather demand is +11.5% vs 5y — but front price is already 'SLIGHTLY OVER' fair value now and Sep/Oct contracts sit in slight backwardation to front, so conviction is capped and targets are kept near the top of the current fair-value bands rather than stretched.
shortTerm.invalidation: A daily close back below ~$3.15 with 1d/1w EMA (50<200) reasserting, or 1-7D weather demand rolling back toward normal, would negate the short-term long.

risks: Storage surplus and bearish weekly build surprises could keep a lid on rallies despite hot weather
